Skills_Dictionary.Purpose: Unit-level medic coverage for suppression units; required civilian paramedic seat on medic units
Skills_Dictionary.Required for Position: Yes for civilian paramedic seat; otherwise unit-level rule
Skills_Dictionary.Notes: Suppression units require at least 1 PARAMEDIC per unit, with preference not Officer

Skills_Dictionary.Purpose: Allows a Firefighter to work higher class in an Engineer seat
Skills_Dictionary.Notes: Not required for personnel already in rank of Engineer

Skills_Dictionary.Purpose: Preference flag for Engineers riding up into Lieutenant seat and Firefighters riding up in Engineers Seat
Skills_Dictionary.Notes: Not an eligibility requirement; only changes preference order

Skills_Dictionary.Required for Position: Yes on Hazmat special team units
Skills_Dictionary.Notes: Technical certification alone does not satisfy Hazmat paid-team minimum

Skills_Dictionary.Counts Toward Minimum Staffing: Yes (special team minimum on Hazmat units)
Skills_Dictionary.Notes: At least 3 assigned members on Hazmat special units must have this flag

Skills_Dictionary.Purpose: Technical certification for TRT / technical rescue work
Skills_Dictionary.Required for Position: Yes on TRT / Heavy Rescue positions
Skills_Dictionary.Notes: Technical certification alone does not satisfy paid-team minimum

Skills_Dictionary.Purpose: Paid TRT / high angle team member flag
Skills_Dictionary.Counts Toward Minimum Staffing: Yes (special team minimum on TRT units)
Skills_Dictionary.Notes: Use where applicable for TRT special team minimums

Skills_Dictionary.Counts Toward Minimum Staffing: Yes (special team minimum on dive units)
Skills_Dictionary.Notes: Use where applicable for dive special team minimums
Required for all Towers, Heavy Rescue 1, Hazmat 1

Skills_Dictionary.Purpose: Required for alternate coverage of tower engineer/driver functions
Skills_Dictionary.Required for Position: Yes for tower engineer seat when applicable
Skills_Dictionary.Notes: Used in conjunction with tower engineer assignments

Skills_Dictionary.Purpose: Assignment eligibility for Woods Truck functions
Critical Incident Stress Management team membership
Required for civilian EMT seat on medic units

Pay_Rules.Applies When: Firefighter is assigned to an Engineer position
Pay_Rules.Units / Scope: All applicable units with Engineer positions
Pay_Rules.Notes: RELIEF_DRIVER governs eligibility; rank-based Engineers do not need RELIEF_DRIVER

Pay_Rules.Applies When: Engineer is assigned to a Lieutenant position
Pay_Rules.Units / Scope: All applicable units with Lieutenant positions
Pay_Rules.Notes: Engineers are eligible with no special qualification; PROMOTIONAL_LIST gives preference only

Pay_Rules.Pay Rule: Working Higher Class as District Chief
Pay_Rules.Applies When: Member is assigned to a District Chief position
Pay_Rules.Who Receives It: Eligible lower-rank member working District Chief
Pay_Rules.Notes: Exact feeder rank / eligibility to be configured separately if needed

Pay_Rules.Pay Rule: Working Higher Class as Assistant Chief
Pay_Rules.Applies When: Member is assigned to an Assistant Chief position
Pay_Rules.Who Receives It: Eligible lower-rank member working Assistant Chief
Pay_Rules.Notes: Exact feeder rank / eligibility to be configured separately if needed

Pay_Rules.Applies When: Member is assigned on a Rescue unit for more than 13 hours during the shift
Pay_Rules.Notes: Applies when assigned on rescue unit beyond 13 hours

Pay_Rules.Applies When: Suppression unit must have at least one PARAMEDIC assigned
Pay_Rules.Notes: Preference is for PARAMEDIC not to be the Officer when another option is available

Pay_Rules.Applies When: Special team units must maintain paid team-member minimum
Pay_Rules.Notes: At least 3 assigned members must be paid members of the respective team; technical certification alone does not satisfy the minimum
